content.theorem: Zig's built-in cross-compilation supports 42+ targets from a single command with zero sysroot configuration
content.proof: zig build -Dtarget=aarch64-linux-gnu — that's it. No sysroot, no cross-compiler toolchain, no Docker container. Zig bundles libc sources for common targets and compiles them on demand. x86_64 → ARM, RISC-V, WASM, macOS, Windows — one toolchain, every target.
content.benchmark: Cross-compile x86→ARM: 3.2 seconds (including libc rebuild). Cross-compile x86→WASM: 1.8 seconds. Native compile: 1.4 seconds.
content.caveat: Libc availability varies by target. musl targets work universally. glibc targets need matching glibc version. Windows MSVC target needs Wine+MSVC for full compatibility.
